description: Sharing 'anonymized' data that retains quasi-identifiers, allowing individuals to be re-identified by linkage.
body: Failure mode: a dataset is shared for analytics after removing names but keeping birth date, ZIP, gender, and rare attributes, so records can be re-identified by linking to external data despite being labeled anonymous. Detection signals: quasi-identifiers remain in the export; no re-identification risk assessment was done; small cells uniquely identify individuals. Do not equate name removal with anonymization. Safer fallback: generalize/suppress quasi-identifiers, run a re-identification risk check, and have privacy review the dataset before release. Risk boundary: advisory only; escalate borderline de-identification to a human privacy decision and never weaken de-identification to preserve analytic detail.
